title: Presigned Browser Uploads, POST Policies, and CORS · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for presigned browser uploads, post policies, and cors and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for least-privilege direct-upload session contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create a one-object upload intent with tenant, generated key, expected size, type, checksum, expiry, status, and replay policy before signing.
Presign PUT with exact region, expiry, key, checksum, content type, encryption, and required header parity.
Use a POST policy when browser-enforced key, size, metadata, and field conditions are required and validate the completed object independently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Upload Session Intent · changed case · Quiz Upload Session Intent
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 5697 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to finalize a direct upload against intent while separating CORS from IAM authorization; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates Upload Session Intent without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Upload Session Intent · changed case · Quiz Upload Session Intent.
